word_list.pronunciation: wɛn wˌaɪl wˈʌnz bˈæk biː tˈɜːnd

senses.definition: used of a situation in which one is busy with something else or is not looking
to gain advantage at the cost of someone else's suffering or loss
to be disloyal and ungrateful to someone who has trusted or supported one

examples.x: I thought we were close friends, but he stabbed me in the back by spreading rumors about me.
She promised to support my project, but then she stabbed me in the back by working against it behind my back.
His business partner stabbed him in the back by stealing company secrets and starting a rival company.
The political candidate claimed to be loyal to his party, but he stabbed them in the back by switching sides right before the election.

senses.definition: a person who has a tendency to deceive or mislead others and is very likely to betray their trust

examples.x: He seemed like a trustworthy friend, but he turned out to be a snake in the grass, betraying our confidence.
Beware of the charming salesman; he may be a snake in the grass, trying to scam you.
She smiled politely, but I sensed a snake in the grass, a hidden agenda behind her words.
Never underestimate the danger of a snake in the grass; they can strike when you least expect it.

senses.definition: to be unfaithful or disloyal to someone so as to gain profit oneself

examples.x: I can't believe my business partner sold me down the river by stealing company funds and disappearing.
He promised to support our cause, but he ended up selling us down the river by revealing our plans to our opponents.
She thought her best friend had her back, but when the opportunity arose, her friend sold her down the river for personal gain.

senses.definition: to not care for someone and leave them all alone
an act that seems kind but with the intention of betraying someone

examples.x: She gave him a Judas kiss, pretending to support his project while secretly working against it.
In politics, a Judas kiss can be a smile for the cameras while undermining your colleagues behind the scenes.
His seemingly friendly offer to help turned out to be a Judas kiss when he stole credit for her idea.
Beware of those who give you a Judas kiss, as they may have ulterior motives.
The partnership turned sour when one partner gave the other a Judas kiss by sharing confidential information with competitors.

word_list.pronunciation: fˈiːd θɹˈoʊ ˌɛsbˈiː tə ðə wˈʊlvz

senses.definition: to make no effort to save or defend someone, particularly when they are being severely criticized or being treated unfairly

examples.x: Instead of helping his friend in need, he decided to feed him to the wolves by revealing his secrets to everyone.
The coach blamed the loss on the rookie player, effectively throwing him to the wolves in front of the team.

senses.definition: to secretely gather information about a person or group in order to expose them to a person of higher authority, often for one's personal gain

examples.x: She knew that her friend was involved in criminal activities, but she didn't want to get directly involved, so she dropped a dime on him to the police.
She found out that her friends had been gossiping about her behind her back.
Betraying a friend by spreading rumors about them behind their back is not a sign of loyalty
They made important decisions about the project behind my back, and I feel left out of the process.

word_list.pronunciation: ɹˈɑːb pˈiːɾɚ tʊ ænd pˈeɪ pˈɔːl

senses.definition: to take from one source or person in order to fulfill an obligation or debt to another source or person, often resulting in a cycle of borrowing or rearranging debts without actually resolving the underlying financial issue

examples.x: Robbing Peter and paying Paul never works, but I guess you have to learn it the hard way.
He's robbing Peter to pay Paul by using his credit card to cover his rent.
The company borrowed from its savings to meet payroll, but now they're robbing Peter to pay Paul.
Taking a loan from one bank to pay off another is just like robbing Peter to pay Paul.
They're robbing Peter to pay Paul with these budget cuts; it's a temporary fix.
